message:
fix(headless_mux_server): restore server_config_serde test after ft-ry224 max_peers addition (ft-7quzz)

ft-ry224 (78721403) added `max_peers: u32` to ServerConfig in
crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:120 as the cap for
the JoinFederation peer registry. The accompanying struct-literal in
the `server_config_serde` test at :1116 was not updated, leaving a
missing-field E0063 build error on the tests target:

    error[E0063]: missing field `max_peers` in initializer of `ServerConfig`

This commit:

- Adds `max_peers: 256` to the test's ServerConfig literal, matching
  the default value the bead's production code path uses. 256 is
  already the federation registry cap ft-ry224 settled on; using it
  here keeps the serde round-trip test faithful to production state
  rather than picking an arbitrary round number.
- Bookkeeping in `.beads/issues.jsonl`:
    * Opens ft-7quzz to document the trivial follow-up caught during
      the ft-ry224 sweep (created 2026-04-22T17:43:29 by jemanuel).
    * Closes ft-f48yx (ingest: overlap inference regression blocks
      frankenterm-core test builds) with close_reason pointing at the
      ingest.rs:2926 overlap_len patch noted in pane8 output. The
      type-annotation patch in ingest.rs that unblocked the test
      build was committed concurrently by another agent, so the bead
      transitions to closed here alongside the mux-server fixture fix
      since they together unblock `cargo test -p frankenterm-core`.

No API change; this is a test-only fixture update plus issue-tracker
state. The build unblock is scoped — ft-kqidb / ft-uigpj / ft-s2u51 /
ft-zv3u9 remain as pre-existing full-feature build blockers.

 {"id":"ft-7cp47","title":"frankenterm-core full-feature lib build fails in vendored mux client and web framework","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:43.079602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:43.079602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7do6c","title":"storage: deferred FTS sync skips seq=0 segment forever (seq > last_indexed_seq off-by-one)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15902-15950 get_unindexed_segments_sync uses WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq > ?2 (strict >). crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:12355 append_segment_sync assigns next_seq = COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0), so the VERY FIRST segment in a fresh pane gets seq=0. Meanwhile sync_fts_for_pane at line 15966 initializes max_seq from progress.last_indexed_seq OR defaults to 0 when no progress exists: 'let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);'. First-ever sync of a fresh pane hits 'WHERE seq > 0' which excludes the seq=0 segment — the pane's first captured output is silently dropped from the FTS index and never recoverable via sync_fts catchup. This is the defect the ft-wk5fo close-note explicitly surfaced but did not fix ('tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix'). Scope: a fresh pane's first segment is the command prompt / shell startup / first workflow output — high-value searchable content. Users get empty search results for anything only in that segment. Fix: distinguish 'never indexed' from 'indexed up through seq=0'. Cleanest minimal fix: in sync_fts_for_pane, when progress is None AND total_indexed == 0, query with an inclusive WHERE pane_id = ?1 (no seq filter) for the first batch. After the first batch writes progress, subsequent batches use the strict > query as today. Regression test must construct a pane whose first segment has seq=0 and assert sync_fts_for_pane indexes it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:14:14.846835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697334Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697066Z","close_reason":"Landed in d37376b0 (fix(storage): unbreak sync_fts_for_pane call site + index seq=0 on first sync). Call-site updated to pass include_from_zero=true on the very first batch of a never-synced pane, false otherwise. had_prior_progress tracks the 'never indexed' sentinel. Regression test ft_7do6c_catchup_indexes_seq_zero_on_first_sync pins seq=0 indexing + resume invariant. Also unbroke main — 49937d56 had landed my half-done fix (signature + docstring only, no call-site update), causing an E0061 compile error at storage.rs:16004. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-7quzz","title":"headless_mux_server: server_config_serde test missing max_peers after ft-ry224 added the field","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:1116 has a ServerConfig struct literal in test server_config_serde that pre-dates ft-ry224's addition of max_peers: u32 (line 120). The test now fails to compile with E0063. Trivial: add max_peers: <value> to the literal.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7s0vw","title":"event_id::detect_clock_anomaly overflows on prev + future_skew_threshold","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs:205\` computes
\`prev + future_skew_threshold\` without overflow protection. In release
builds u64 wraps silently; in debug builds the unchecked add panics.
Either way, a pathological or adversarial \`prev\` value breaks the
future-skew branch.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`detect_clock_anomaly(current, prev, threshold)\` where
   \`prev + threshold\` overflows \`u64\`.
2. Easy reach: replay/import flows feeding \`recorded_at_ms = u64::MAX\` from
   a corrupt fixture, fuzzer, or attacker-controlled input. Also reachable
   via legitimate-but-distant clock sources (UNIX epoch micros around year
   586,524 if anyone reaches that, or any monotonic clock that has been
   bumped by a process bug into the high u64 range).
3. **Release build:** \`prev + threshold\` wraps to a tiny value. The
   condition \`current > tiny_value\` is now true for almost any \`current\`,
   so the function returns \`is_anomaly: true\` for every subsequent valid
   timestamp — flooding telemetry with false-positive "future skew"
   reports and potentially triggering downstream remediation that should
   not have fired.
4. **Debug build:** unchecked add panics (\`attempt to add with overflow\`),
   killing whatever thread was processing the recorder pipeline.
5. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` always updates the baseline
   ("Updates the baseline regardless") — so a single malicious or
   transiently-high \`current\` value sticks around and continues to
   poison overflow checks against subsequent observations.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer in release; thread-killing panic in debug.
- Adversary-reachable via fixture/replay/fuzz inputs.
- Same arithmetic-poison family as ft-761tz (disk_pressure NaN) and
  ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure NaN): a hostile or pathological
  numeric input cascades into permanently-wrong tier/anomaly outputs
  with no self-recovery.

## Fix sketch

1. Replace \`prev + future_skew_threshold\` with \`prev.checked_add(threshold)\`.
2. On overflow, treat as "no future-skew anomaly" — we cannot prove a
   timestamp is too far in the future relative to a near-MAX baseline,
   so the only sound default is \"don't flag\". Same disposition as
   ft-761tz: when the math itself becomes unreliable, fail toward
   silence rather than toward false positives.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) overflow with threshold=1 and
   prev=u64::MAX → no anomaly, no panic; (b) overflow with threshold=
   u64::MAX and small prev → no anomaly, no panic; (c) the existing
   future-skew path still fires when math is in range.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` baseline-update behavior is
intentional and out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:32.110899Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255609Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4431483f: detect_clock_anomaly now uses prev.checked_add(future_skew_threshold), failing silent on overflow rather than panicking (debug) or wrapping (release). 5 regression tests added covering (current=prev=u64::MAX, threshold=1), (huge threshold), (boundary u64::MAX × u64::MAX), (in-range path still fires), and (tracker survives MAX baseline). All 53 event_id tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-761tz (NaN) and ft-yskcu (NaN cascade).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-84ulk","title":"workflow runner releases pane lock on pre-start cx cancellation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:27.230187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.275236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.274845Z","close_reason":"Released pane lock and marked execution failed when run_workflow_with_cx sees a pre-start cancelled child context; added regression coverage for the persisted-record + lock cleanup path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-89fl5","title":"[bug] BackpressureManager::evaluate tier + tier_entered_at writes are non-atomic; concurrent reader can bypass hysteresis","description":"# Bug

backpressure.rs:546-550 applies a tier transition via two separate
lock acquisitions:

    *self.current_tier.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = proposed;
    *self
        .tier_entered_at
        .write()
        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Instant::now();

Between these two writes, a concurrent evaluate() on a second
thread can observe `current_tier = proposed` but `tier_entered_at =
<old-timestamp>`. That thread's hysteresis check at line 540:

    let entered = *self.tier_entered_at.read()...;
    let elapsed_ms = u64::try_from(entered.elapsed().as_millis())...;
    if elapsed_ms < self.config.hysteresis_ms {
        return None;
    }

uses the STALE `entered` value. Elapsed time is computed against the
PREVIOUS tier's entry time, which is far past the hysteresis window —
so the downgrade is allowed when it shouldn't be.

# Scenario

1. T=0: tier=Green, entered_at=T0.
2. T=100s: T1 evaluate() proposes Red (upgrade). Writes
   current_tier=Red. Scheduler pauses T1 before `tier_entered_at`
   write.
3. T=100.001s: T2 evaluate() reads current_tier=Red, proposes Green
   (downgrade). Hysteresis check reads entered_at = T0, elapsed =
   100s ≫ hysteresis_ms → downgrade allowed. Writes
   current_tier=Green.
4. T=100.002s: T1 resumes, writes tier_entered_at=100s.

Final state: current_tier=Green, tier_entered_at=100s. Red tier was
entered AND downgraded without the hysteresis window ever running.

# When does this matter

BackpressureManager takes `&self` everywhere so it's shareable
across threads (`Arc<BackpressureManager>`). Today there's only one
active evaluator task in the runtime (runtime.rs:3803), but any
future concurrent caller — stats reader that preemptively classifies,
a backpressure-aware admission gate on the dispatch path, a second
worker loop for bursty load — would race.

Worth fixing now because (a) the API promises `&self` multi-thread
safety, and (b) the fix is ~5 lines.

# Fix

Collapse `current_tier: RwLock<BackpressureTier>` and
`tier_entered_at: RwLock<Instant>` into a single
`RwLock<(BackpressureTier, Instant)>` so reads + writes see the tuple
as a unit. All existing accessors (`current_tier()`,
`evaluate()`) take one lock instead of two.

# Test

`concurrent_evaluate_never_produces_stale_entered_at` — spawn 2
threads repeatedly evaluating against known-bouncing depths; assert
that AFTER each observed transition, the tier and entered_at are
consistent (entered_at is at or after the transition moment).

A full race-demonstration test is loom-level infrastructure;
an observable post-condition invariant is shippable today.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:29.956478Z","created_by":"pane3-bp-atomic","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","concurrency","hysteresis"]}
@@ -125,7 +126,7 @@

 {"id":"ft-ep8ba","title":"frankenterm-core test build blocked by undefined exit_reason in runtime fallback path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:17.170480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:19.020880Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:19.020329Z","close_reason":"Fixed in cacb82c7; vendored streaming capture now drains subscription deltas instead of reading undefined exit_reason. Verification: rch cargo test blocked on remote CC=/opt/homebrew/... path missing on Linux worker; local cargo test reached unrelated existing compile errors in vendored/mux_client.rs and web_framework.rs after this slice compiled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-fq73h","title":"robot_types contract coverage omits current mission/tx error codes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:14:29.145581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:22:41.332099Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:22:41.331762Z","close_reason":"Landed in 8f5afe60","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fs42k","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses event.timestamp_ms (parallel ft-dijpe miss)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027\` passes
\`event.timestamp_ms\` (caller-supplied via the public
\`OutboundEvent::with_timestamp_ms\` builder, line 284) to
\`check_and_record\` as the dedup eviction clock — the exact shape that
ft-dijpe just fixed in the inbound bridge.

The ft-dijpe defect description even claimed *"Outbound bridge doesn't
have this shape — it uses its own \`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now()\`
for all internal state."* That claim is wrong. The outbound bridge's
constructor (line 245) does use \`SystemTime::now()\` to seed
\`timestamp_ms\` by default, but:

1. The public \`with_timestamp_ms\` builder (line 284) lets any caller
   override that default to an arbitrary value.
2. \`route_event\` line 1027 then passes that caller-controlled value
   directly to \`OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record\`, which calls
   \`evict_expired(now_ms)\` and pops anything whose
   \`now_ms - inserted_at_ms > ttl_ms\`.
3. Same replay-bypass attack as ft-dijpe: a caller submitting two
   copies of the same correlation_id with the second copy's
   \`timestamp_ms = ttl_ms + 1\` triggers eviction of the first entry
   and the replay is treated as new.

## Concrete failure path

1. Internal caller (workflow handler, retry loop, test harness) builds
   an \`OutboundEvent\` and calls \`with_timestamp_ms\` to override the
   default \`SystemTime::now()\` (legitimate reason: replaying historical
   events for backfill, fixture-driven tests, or a buggy clock-advance
   helper).
2. First dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=0\`.
3. Second dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1\`.
4. \`evict_expired\` sees \`now_ms=ttl+1 > inserted_at=0+ttl\` → pops X.
5. \`check_and_record\` finds cache empty, inserts a new X, returns
   \`true\` → second dispatch routes as if new. All downstream
   side-effects fire twice (slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR
   comment, credential broker call, etc.).

Trust model is weaker than inbound's — outbound events are
internally constructed rather than attacker-supplied — but the
defense-in-depth argument applies. A buggy internal caller that
mis-sets \`timestamp_ms\` should not silently bypass dedup, and
symmetry with the inbound bridge's hardening makes the codebase
easier to reason about.

## Severity rationale

- Same shape as ft-dijpe (silent dedup bypass), one severity level
  lower because the trust model is internal-only.
- Reachable today via any internal caller misusing
  \`with_timestamp_ms\`. Future caller bugs are likelier than
  attacker reach.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-dijpe fix: in \`route_event\` line 1027, compute
\`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()\`
and pass that to \`check_and_record\` instead of \`event.timestamp_ms\`.
The \`event.timestamp_ms\` field stays useful as an analytics signal
(end-to-end source→sink latency, dispatch-history correlation) but
must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

## Regression test

\`bridge_dedup_ignores_event_timestamp_ms_for_eviction\`: dispatch X
with \`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=0\`, then dispatch X with
\`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2\`. Assert the second is
\`deduplicated=true\`. Pre-fix the second returns \`deduplicated=false\`
because evict_expired with the spoofed future-timestamp pops the live
entry.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs\` only.
The line-1080 \`let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms\` (used downstream for
\`created_at_ms\`, \`broker_checked_at_ms\`, \`dispatched_at_ms\`) is a
separate concern — those telemetry timestamps arguably should also
be SystemTime::now() but that's a wider change. This bead covers
ONLY the dedup eviction clock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:25:28.629996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.140282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.139925Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-ptjlp (same defect, same line, parallel filing race). Sibling agent already applied the SystemTime::now() fix in working tree at connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1037-1044 with their own ft-ptjlp tag in the inline comment. Closing this bead so ft-ptjlp owns the fix and the test work. Reviewed their fix — it's identical to what I would have written (SystemTime::now() → dedup_now_ms → check_and_record), correctly preserves event.timestamp_ms for downstream telemetry uses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -227,7 +228,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-ry224","title":"headless_mux_server: JoinFederation has no peer-count cap (unbounded DoS) + clobbers first_seen_at on rejoin","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:402-416 RemoteRequest::JoinFederation blindly inserts into self.peers without checking a cap, and overwrites the existing PeerInfo entirely on every re-join — losing first_seen_at. Two distinct defects:

1. **Unbounded peer-map growth (DoS)**: ServerConfig has max_connections (for clients) and max_panes, but NO max_peers. A hostile or buggy counterparty spamming JoinFederation with distinct node_ids grows self.peers without bound, eventually OOM'ing the mux server. prune_unreachable_peers only fires after peer_timeout_ms + an external caller — a burst of N spurious JoinFederation requests can bloat the map before any prune runs. Parallel shape to ft-kfy99 (tmux_cc line cap), ft-phz7x (codec oversize tagged_len), ft-8ihuz (scrollback unbounded read).

2. **first_seen_at lost on rejoin**: Every JoinFederation fully replaces PeerInfo at line 404-414, including setting first_seen_at to epoch_ms(). A peer that legitimately rejoins (e.g. partition heal after the Heartbeat ft-lekgj path tells it to re-JoinFederation) loses its original first_seen_at — making 'how long has this peer been federated' observability lie.

Fix: add max_peers: u32 to ServerConfig (default 512, twice max_connections as headroom). In JoinFederation, if the node_id is NEW and self.peers.len() >= max_peers, return RemoteResponse::Error { code: "peer_registry_full" }. If the node_id already exists, preserve first_seen_at from the existing entry and only update the re-join-time fields (node, status, last_heartbeat_at, capabilities). Regression tests: (a) 512-peer cap rejects the 513th distinct JoinFederation with peer_registry_full; (b) a rejoin keeps first_seen_at of the original insert.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:39:20.982908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:41:25.477529Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:41:25.476570Z","close_reason":"Landed in 78721403 (fix(headless_mux_server): cap JoinFederation peer registry + preserve first_seen_at on rejoin). Added ServerConfig.max_peers (default 512) + JoinFederation rejects new peers past cap with peer_registry_full; re-joins of known node_ids still succeed (they don't consume a slot). Also preserves existing first_seen_at on rejoin. Two regression tests pin both contracts. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-s2u51","title":"asupersync dep graph split: crates.io 0.2.9 + git HEAD both present, breaks fastapi/fastmcp bridges","description":"cargo check reports 'there are multiple different versions of crate asupersync in the dependency graph' for web_framework.rs:180 (ResponseBody::stream needs asupersync 0.2.9's Stream, code has git Stream) and mcp_tools.rs:4759 (McpContext::new needs fastmcp_rust's asupersync Cx, code passes git asupersync's Cx). Blocks default-feature build of frankenterm-core. Root cause: fastmcp_rust and fastapi-core pin asupersync 0.2.9 from crates.io while the top-level project pins git HEAD via [patch] — the transitive deps don't see the patch. Fix: either bump fastmcp_rust/fastapi-core to match, or add patch entries that reach them transitively.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:50.396241Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:50.396241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-s4c6y","title":"retry::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt panics on NaN backoff_factor or jitter_percent (no fail-closed)","description":"RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt computes base_ms = initial_ms as f64 * backoff_factor.powi(exp); then jitter_range = base_ms * jitter_percent; then rng.random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range). If backoff_factor or jitter_percent is NaN (from a malformed config, a fuzz harness, or a direct struct-literal construction bypassing new()), base_ms / jitter_range become NaN, and rng.random_range(NaN..=NaN) panics. Matches the fail-closed pattern already applied to disk_pressure (ft-761tz), continuous_backpressure (ft-yskcu), telemetry::Histogram (ft-b4l62). Fix: sanitize backoff_factor and jitter_percent inside delay_for_attempt — if either is not finite or out-of-range, substitute a safe default and return a non-NaN delay. Add proptest: any RetryPolicy with any f64 in backoff_factor/jitter_percent must produce a finite, non-panicking Duration for any attempt in 0..=63.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:09.137669Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:18.432344Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:18.431974Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-s4myu","title":"storage: foreign_keys PRAGMA not applied on connection open for up-to-date DBs — CASCADE deletes silently skip","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` `StorageHandle::with_config` (line 6002) opens the writer connection via `Connection::open(&db_path_owned)` and then calls `initialize_schema(&conn)`. The `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` statement is ONLY present inside `SCHEMA_SQL` (line 104) which is executed via `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)` at line 4349/4375.

For an UP-TO-DATE database (user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION, the common case on every frankenterm restart), `initialize_schema` short-circuits at line 4341-4344:

```rust
if current == SCHEMA_VERSION {
    // Already up to date
    ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
    return Ok(());
}
```

...returning WITHOUT executing SCHEMA_SQL. SQLite's `PRAGMA foreign_keys` is PER-CONNECTION — it must be re-issued on every new connection. Since the ONLY place that sets it is the schema-init path, every writer connection on an existing DB has `foreign_keys = OFF` (the SQLite default).

## Impact (HIGH)

Every ON DELETE CASCADE in the schema silently fails to fire. Concrete example:

```rust
fn prune_session_checkpoints_sync(conn, session_id, retention) -> Result<usize> {
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1 AND id NOT IN (...)")
}
```

`mux_pane_state` rows reference `session_checkpoints(id) ON DELETE CASCADE` at schema line 646. On an up-to-date DB, the DELETE removes the checkpoint row but leaves ORPHAN mux_pane_state rows. Over time these accumulate indefinitely.

Same pattern affects 15+ other references in SCHEMA_SQL (output_segments→panes, events→panes, workflow_executions→panes, etc.). Every `delete_session`, retention sweep, and explicit pane cleanup path leaks orphan rows.

Silent data corruption accumulates across restarts. A fresh-DB test suite (which runs SCHEMA_SQL) would never catch this.

## Fix

Apply `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` unconditionally on every connection open, after `Connection::open` and before the initialize_schema short-circuit. One-liner:

```rust
let conn = Connection::open(&db_path_owned)?;
check_and_recover_wal(&conn, &db_path_owned)?;
conn.pragma_update(None, "foreign_keys", true)
    .map_err(|e| StorageError::Database(format!("Failed to enable foreign keys: {e}")))?;
initialize_schema(&conn)?;
```

This is idempotent (the pragma is a per-connection toggle) and costs O(1) per open.

## Tests

- `prune_session_checkpoints_cascades_mux_pane_state`: open storage on pre-populated DB, call prune with retention=0, assert every mux_pane_state row for deleted checkpoints is gone (vs current behavior where they persist).
- `fresh_db_and_existing_db_both_enforce_foreign_keys`: open on fresh + reopen on existing, in each case attempt to INSERT a mux_pane_state with a non-existent checkpoint_id, assert SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY.

## Severity

HIGH. Silent data corruption, invisible to fresh-DB tests, accumulates across restarts. 15+ FK definitions in SCHEMA_SQL silently no-op for existing databases. Fix is one line.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:18.699462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:37.071424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Fix is one line.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:18.699462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:44:16.969109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:44:16.968817Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn — premise was wrong. The bundled SQLite that libsqlite3-sys builds compiles with `-DSQLITE_DEFAULT_FOREIGN_KEYS=1` (libsqlite3-sys-0.36.0/build.rs:127), so every rusqlite Connection::open starts with foreign_keys=1 by default. Empirically confirmed by running the reopen test: conn2 had PRAGMA foreign_keys returning 1 (not 0). The PRAGMA statement inside SCHEMA_SQL is redundant-not-missing. CASCADE deletes DO fire on fresh connections even when initialize_schema short-circuits. Reverted the uncommitted storage.rs edit. Note: if frankenterm ever switched to non-bundled SQLite or moved off libsqlite3-sys, this defense would be needed — but under the current Cargo.toml ('rusqlite = { features = ["bundled", ...] }' at Cargo.toml:246) there is no live defect.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-s6l5b","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin match_rule_glob invariants under proptest (empty patterns, star-wildcards, length preservation)","description":"# Gap

events.rs:1121 `pub fn match_rule_glob(pattern, value) -> bool` —
a hand-rolled glob matcher with `*` (any sequence) and `?` (any
single char) support. 19 inline example tests at events.rs:2827+ cover
specific cases. No proptest / property-based coverage exists.

# Risk

Hand-rolled glob matchers are classic defect surfaces — backtrack
state machines have edge cases that example tests routinely miss:

1. Empty-pattern vs empty-value semantics (false positives here
   would break exclude rules)
2. Pattern of N `?`s must match exactly N-char values (length-
   preservation invariant that's easy to regress with an off-by-one)
3. `"*"` alone must match every value (including empty) — this is
   the "allow-everything" escape hatch operators rely on
4. Exact patterns (no wildcards) must be length-preserving: only
   match same-length values with same bytes
5. Idempotence: `match_rule_glob(x, x) == true` for any exact pattern x

# Fix

New proptest suite in events.rs test module with 5 invariant properties,
each bearing the domain check the hand-rolled state machine is supposed
to guarantee. Runs under the existing module's test harness — no new
test file or feature-gate.

# Catches

- Backtrack-reset off-by-one at events.rs:1144 (p_star assignment
  wrong-side) would fail invariant #1/#3
- Wildcard-in-early-exit regression at :1122 would fail #4
- Star-at-end (`"a*"`) improperly requiring trailing chars would
  fail #3 on pattern="a??" value="abc"

Shipping as a net-positive guard, not a known-bug fix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:36.694100Z","created_by":"pane3-glob-proptest","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:29:58.443230Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:29:58.442992Z","close_reason":"Landed in f3e3f68c (test(events): add proptest coverage for match_rule_glob invariants). Pinned 5 properties covering exact-pattern self-match, star-matches-everything, N-questions length preservation, empty-pattern empty-value only, and reflexivity over wildcard-free values. Verified: 8 passed / 0 failed (5 proptests × default cases + 3 example tests) on cargo test --lib --features asupersync-runtime match_rule_glob, 0.09s. EXIT=0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["events","proptest","testing"]}
